name: Sausage Triangles
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook pork sausage and mushrooms until the meat is well browned and the mushrooms are tender, stirring occasionally.
2. Using a slotted spoon, remove the sausage mixture to paper towels to drain.
3. In a large bowl, mix the drained sausage mixture with shredded cheese.
4. Cut phyllo lengthwise into 1-inch wide strips using a knife.
5. Place strips on waxed paper; then cover with slightly damp paper towel to prevent the phyllo from drying out.
6. On the work surface, place one strip of phyllo and brush lightly with melted butter.
7. Place about 1 teaspoon of sausage mixture at the end of the strip.
8. Fold one corner of the strip diagonally over the filling so that the short edge meets the long edge of the strip, forming a right angle.
9. Continue folding over at right angles until you reach the end of the strip to form a triangular package.
10. Place the package seam-side down in a 15-1/2-inch by 10-1/2-inch jelly-roll pan; brush with butter. Repeat with remaining phyllo strips and sausage mixture.
11. If not serving immediately, cover the pan with foil and refrigerate.
To serve:
12. Preheat oven to 425°F. Bake triangles for 15 minutes or until golden. Makes about 3-1/2 dozen hors d'oeuvres. Each triangle: About 50 calories, 4 g fat, 5 mg cholesterol, 90 mg sodium.
13. To freeze and use up to 1 month later: Place the unbaked triangles in a freezer container with waxed paper between each layer; seal; label; and freeze. About 35 minutes before serving, preheat oven to 425°F. Arrange frozen triangles on an un-greased jelly-roll pan and bake for 20 minutes or until golden.
